task_completion.description: Verify that the sales_data table is imported and the required calculated columns are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Data/Fields pane on the right, the 'sales_data' table should be visible and expanded, showing fields for 'Day_Name' and 'Day_Number' (typically with a calculated column icon next to them).

task_completion.description: Verify that the Total_Sales measure is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Data/Fields pane, there should be a 'Total_Sales' field with a calculator icon next to it, indicating it is a DAX measure.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report page is named 'Heatmap'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The page tab at the bottom of the Power BI canvas should be renamed to 'Heatmap'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Card visual is created displaying the Total_Sales measure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Card visual (displaying a single large number) should be visible on the canvas. The value should be approximately 5.02M (representing the total sales sum of 5,019,265.23).

task_completion.description: Verify that a Matrix visual is created with Day_Name on rows and Product_Category on columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Matrix visual (pivot table) should be visible on the canvas. The row headers should display days of the week (e.g., Monday, Tuesday) and the column headers should display product categories (Clothing, Electronics, Food, Furniture).

task_completion.description: Verify that background color conditional formatting is applied to the Matrix values to create a heatmap.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interior value cells of the Matrix visual must have varying background colors (e.g., gradients of color from light to dark) based on the numerical values in the cells.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the Power BI Desktop GUI to create the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Power BI Desktop application interface being actively used, with menus, panes, and dialogs open, rather than a terminal or script editor.

integrity.description: Verify that the visuals are genuine Power BI components and not mocked with static text boxes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Matrix and Card should look like standard Power BI visuals with proper alignment, formatting, and realistic data values (e.g., Card showing ~5.02M), and the Visualizations pane should show the correct visual type selected when active.

integrity.description: Verify that the file is saved with the correct name.
integrity.visual_evidence: The title bar of the Power BI Desktop application should read 'Weekday_Heatmap - Power BI Desktop' or a save dialog should show this filename.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 1000 rows. The total sum of Sales_Amount is exactly 5,019,265.23, so the Card visual should display approximately 5.02M. The Product_Category column contains exactly four unique values: Clothing, Electronics, Food, and Furniture; these must appear as the column headers in the Matrix visual. The Matrix visual must have background color conditional formatting applied to its value cells to create a heatmap effect.
